+// Test that dotenv-override setting allows .env to override existing environment variables
+#[test]
+fn dotenv_override_enabled() {
+  Test::new()
+    .justfile(
+      "
+        set dotenv-load
+        set dotenv-override
+
+        echo:
+          echo $DOTENV_KEY
+      ",
+    )
+    .write(".env", "DOTENV_KEY=dotenv-value")
+    .env("DOTENV_KEY", "env-value")
+    .stdout("dotenv-value\n")
+    .stderr("echo $DOTENV_KEY\n")
+    .run();
+}
+
+// Test that dotenv-override := true works explicitly
+#[test]
+fn dotenv_override_explicit_true() {
+  Test::new()
+    .justfile(
+      "
+        set dotenv-load
+        set dotenv-override := true
+
+        echo:
+          echo $DOTENV_KEY
+      ",
+    )
+    .write(".env", "DOTENV_KEY=dotenv-value")
+    .env("DOTENV_KEY", "env-value")
+    .stdout("dotenv-value\n")
+    .stderr("echo $DOTENV_KEY\n")
+    .run();
+}
+
+// Test that dotenv-override := false preserves existing behavior (env wins)
+#[test]
+fn dotenv_override_explicit_false() {
+  Test::new()
+    .justfile(
+      "
+        set dotenv-load
+        set dotenv-override := false
+
+        echo:
+          echo $DOTENV_KEY
+      ",
+    )
+    .write(".env", "DOTENV_KEY=dotenv-value")
+    .env("DOTENV_KEY", "env-value")
+    .stdout("env-value\n")
+    .stderr("echo $DOTENV_KEY\n")
+    .run();
+}
+
+// Test that without dotenv-override, existing env vars are not overridden (default behavior)
+#[test]
+fn dotenv_no_override_default() {
+  Test::new()
+    .justfile(
+      "
+        set dotenv-load
+
+        echo:
+          echo $DOTENV_KEY
+      ",
+    )
+    .write(".env", "DOTENV_KEY=dotenv-value")
+    .env("DOTENV_KEY", "env-value")
+    .stdout("env-value\n")
+    .stderr("echo $DOTENV_KEY\n")
+    .run();
+}
+
+// Test that dotenv-override works with USER variable (the original use case)
+#[test]
+fn dotenv_override_user_variable() {
+  Test::new()
+    .justfile(
+      "
+        set dotenv-load
+        set dotenv-override
+
+        echo:

+      ",

+    .env("USER", "original-user")
+    .stdout("custom-user\n")
+    .stderr("echo $USER\n")
+    .run();
+}

use super::*;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub(crate) enum Setting<'src> {
  AllowDuplicateRecipes(bool),
  AllowDuplicateVariables(bool),
  DotenvFilename(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvLoad(bool),
  DotenvPath(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvRequired(bool),
  Export(bool),
  Fallback(bool),
  IgnoreComments(bool),
  NoExitMessage(bool),
  PositionalArguments(bool),
  Quiet(bool),
  ScriptInterpreter(Interpreter<'src>),
  Shell(Interpreter<'src>),
  Tempdir(StringLiteral<'src>),
  Unstable(bool),
  WindowsPowerShell(bool),
  WindowsShell(Interpreter<'src>),
  WorkingDirectory(StringLiteral<'src>),
}

impl Display for Setting<'_> {
  fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter) -> fmt::Result {
    match self {
      Self::AllowDuplicateRecipes(value)
      | Self::AllowDuplicateVariables(value)
      | Self::DotenvLoad(value)
      | Self::DotenvRequired(value)
      | Self::Export(value)
      | Self::Fallback(value)
      | Self::IgnoreComments(value)
      | Self::NoExitMessage(value)
      | Self::PositionalArguments(value)
      | Self::Quiet(value)
      | Self::Unstable(value)
      | Self::WindowsPowerShell(value) => write!(f, "{value}"),
      Self::ScriptInterpreter(shell) | Self::Shell(shell) | Self::WindowsShell(shell) => {
        write!(f, "[{shell}]")
      }
      Self::DotenvFilename(value)
      | Self::DotenvPath(value)
      | Self::Tempdir(value)
      | Self::WorkingDirectory(value) => {
        write!(f, "{value}")
      }
    }
  }
}

## Summary: Dotenv Functionality in the Codebase

I've completed a thorough investigation of the dotenv implementation. Here are my findings:

---

### 1. **Files Related to Dotenv Functionality**

Key files:
- **/testbed/src/load_dotenv.rs** - Core dotenv loading logic
- **/testbed/src/setting.rs** - Setting enum definitions including dotenv-related settings
- **/testbed/src/settings.rs** - Settings struct and configuration from set directives
- **/testbed/src/parser.rs** - Parsing logic for `set` directives (lines 1082-1158)
- **/testbed/src/keyword.rs** - Keywords including dotenv-related keywords
- **/testbed/src/config.rs** - CLI argument handling for dotenv options
- **/testbed/src/command_ext.rs** - Environment variable export mechanism
- **/testbed/src/justfile.rs** - Integration point where dotenv is loaded and used
- **/testbed/tests/dotenv.rs** - Comprehensive test suite for dotenv functionality

---

### 2. **How Settings Are Defined and Used (The "set" Directive)**

**Settings Enum** (`/testbed/src/setting.rs`):
```rust
pub(crate) enum Setting<'src> {
  DotenvFilename(StringLiteral<'src>),   // Custom .env filename
  DotenvLoad(bool),                      // Enable/disable loading
  DotenvPath(StringLiteral<'src>),       // Custom path to .env file
  DotenvRequired(bool),                  // Require .env to exist
  // ... other settings
}
```

**Settings Struct** (`/testbed/src/settings.rs` lines 8-30):
```rust
pub(crate) struct Settings<'src> {
  pub(crate) dotenv_filename: Option<String>,
  pub(crate) dotenv_load: bool,
  pub(crate) dotenv_path: Option<PathBuf>,
  pub(crate) dotenv_required: bool,
  // ... other settings
}
```

**Parsing Logic** (`/testbed/src/parser.rs` lines 1104-1158):

The parser supports two types of settings:

1. **Boolean settings** (no `:=` required or with `:= true/false`):
   - `set dotenv-load` → treats as `true`
   - `set dotenv-load := true`
   - `set dotenv-load := false`
   - Also applies to: `dotenv-required`

2. **Value settings** (requires `:=`):
   - `set dotenv-filename := ".env.custom"`
   - `set dotenv-path := "path/to/.env"`

The `parse_set_bool()` function (lines 1082-1101) handles boolean parsing:
- If no `:=` is present, it defaults to `true`
- Otherwise, it expects either `true` or `false` identifier

**Configuration Mapping** (`/testbed/src/settings.rs` lines 33-99):
The `Settings::from_table()` method maps parsed `Setting` enum values to the `Settings` struct fields.

---

### 3. **How Environment Variables Are Loaded from .env Files**

**Loading Process** (`/testbed/src/load_dotenv.rs` lines 3-59):

```rust
pub(crate) fn load_dotenv(
  config: &Config,
  settings: &Settings,
  working_directory: &Path,
) -> RunResult<'static, BTreeMap<String, String>>
```

**Loading Strategy** (priority order):

1. **Skip if not needed** (lines 18-24): If `dotenv_load=false`, `dotenv_filename=None`, `dotenv_path=None`, and `dotenv_required=false`, return empty map.

2. **Check explicit path first** (lines 26-31): If `dotenv_path` is set, load from that specific file path (relative to working directory).

3. **Search for filename** (lines 35-40): Walk up the directory tree from `working_directory` searching for `dotenv_filename` (defaults to `.env`).

4. **Error handling** (lines 42-46): If `dotenv_required=true` and no file found, throw `Error::DotenvRequired`.

**File Loading** (lines 49-59):
```rust
fn load_from_file(path: &Path) -> RunResult<'static, BTreeMap<String, String>> {
  let iter = dotenvy::from_path_iter(path)?;  // Uses dotenvy crate
  let mut dotenv = BTreeMap::new();
  for result in iter {
    let (key, value) = result?;
    if env::var_os(&key).is_none() {  // Only set if not already in env
      dotenv.insert(key, value);
    }
  }
  Ok(dotenv)
}
```

**Key behavior**: Existing environment variables take precedence over .env values (line 54).

**Integration Point** (`/testbed/src/justfile.rs` lines 90-94):
```rust
let dotenv = if config.load_dotenv {
  load_dotenv(config, &self.settings, &search.working_directory)?
} else {
  BTreeMap::new()
};
```

**Export to Commands** (`/testbed/src/command_ext.rs` lines 22-38):
```rust
fn export(
  &mut self,
  settings: &Settings,
  dotenv: &BTreeMap<String, String>,
  scope: &Scope,
  unexports: &HashSet<String>,
) -> &mut Command {
  for (name, value) in dotenv {
    self.env(name, value);  // Sets environment variable
  }
  // ... exports scope variables
}
```

---

### 4. **Existing Tests for Dotenv Functionality**

**Test File**: `/testbed/tests/dotenv.rs` (410 lines)

**Key Test Categories**:

1. **Basic loading**:
   - `dotenv()` - Load root .env when subdir doesn't have one
   - `set_false()` - Disabling with `set dotenv-load := false`
   - `set_implicit()` - Implicit true with `set dotenv-load`
   - `set_true()` - Explicit true with `set dotenv-load := true`

2. **Settings tests**:
   - `dotenv_required()` - Error when file missing with `set dotenv-required`
   - `path_resolves()` - `--dotenv-path` CLI flag
   - `filename_resolves()` - `--dotenv-filename` CLI flag
   - `can_set_dotenv_filename_from_justfile()` - Setting in recipe file
   - `can_set_dotenv_path_from_justfile()` - Setting in recipe file

3. **Priority/Override tests**:
   - `program_argument_has_priority_for_dotenv_filename()` - CLI overrides justfile
   - `program_argument_has_priority_for_dotenv_path()` - CLI overrides justfile
   - `filename_flag_overwrites_no_load()` - CLI flag overrides disabled loading
   - `path_flag_overwrites_no_load()` - CLI flag overrides disabled loading

4. **Variable usage tests**:
   - `dotenv_variable_in_recipe()` - Using in recipe commands
   - `dotenv_variable_in_backtick()` - Using in backtick expressions
   - `dotenv_variable_in_function_in_recipe()` - Using with `env_var()` function
   - `dotenv_variable_in_function_in_backtick()` - Using with `env_var()` in backticks

5. **Environment override**:
   - `dotenv_env_var_override()` - OS environment variables override .env values
   - `no_dotenv()` - `--no-dotenv` CLI flag disables loading

6. **Path resolution**:
   - `dotenv_path_is_relative_to_working_directory()` - Relative to working dir
   - `dotenv_path_usable_from_subdir()` - Accessible from subdirectories

---

### **CLI Arguments** (`/testbed/src/config.rs`)

- `--dotenv-path` / `-E`: Specify custom .env file path
- `--dotenv-filename`: Specify custom .env filename
- `--no-dotenv`: Disable .env loading completely
- Environment variable: `JUST_NO_DOTENV` (affects `--no-dotenv`)

---

### **Priority Order (Lowest to Highest)**

1. Default values in `Settings::default()`
2. Values from `set` directives in justfile
3. Command-line arguments (`--dotenv-path`, `--dotenv-filename`)

This is reflected in `/testbed/src/load_dotenv.rs` lines 8-16:
```rust
let dotenv_filename = config.dotenv_filename.as_ref()
  .or(settings.dotenv_filename.as_ref());

let dotenv_path = config.dotenv_path.as_ref()
  .or(settings.dotenv_path.as_ref());
```

---

### **Key Behaviors**

1. **Environment variables take precedence**: OS env vars override .env file values
2. **Implicit true for booleans**: `set dotenv-load` equals `set dotenv-load := true`
3. **Walking directory tree**: When using filename search, searches from working directory up to root
4. **Command-line overrides**: CLI flags override justfile settings
5. **Uses `dotenvy` crate**: For actual .env file parsing

- **/testbed/src/command_ext.rs** - Environment variable export mechanism
- **/testbed/src/justfile.rs** - Integration point where dotenv is loaded and used
- **/testbed/tests/dotenv.rs** - Comprehensive test suite for dotenv functionality

---

### 2. **How Settings Are Defined and Used (The "set" Directive)**

**Settings Enum** (`/testbed/src/setting.rs`):
```rust
pub(crate) enum Setting<'src> {
  DotenvFilename(StringLiteral<'src>),   // Custom .env filename
  DotenvLoad(bool),                      // Enable/disable loading
  DotenvPath(StringLiteral<'src>),       // Custom path to .env file
  DotenvRequired(bool),                  // Require .env to exist
  // ... other settings
}
```

**Settings Struct** (`/testbed/src/settings.rs` lines 8-30):
```rust
pub(crate) struct Settings<'src> {
  pub(crate) dotenv_filename: Option<String>,
  pub(crate) dotenv_load: bool,
  pub(crate) dotenv_path: Option<PathBuf>,
  pub(crate) dotenv_required: bool,
  // ... other settings
}
```

**Parsing Logic** (`/testbed/src/parser.rs` lines 1104-1158):

The parser supports two types of settings:

1. **Boolean settings** (no `:=` required or with `:= true/false`):
   - `set dotenv-load` → treats as `true`
   - `set dotenv-load := true`
   - `set dotenv-load := false`
   - Also applies to: `dotenv-required`

2. **Value settings** (requires `:=`):
   - `set dotenv-filename := ".env.custom"`
   - `set dotenv-path := "path/to/.env"`

The `parse_set_bool()` function (lines 1082-1101) handles boolean parsing:
- If no `:=` is present, it defaults to `true`
- Otherwise, it expects either `true` or `false` identifier

**Configuration Mapping** (`/testbed/src/settings.rs` lines 33-99):
The `Settings::from_table()` method maps parsed `Setting` enum values to the `Settings` struct fields.

---

### 3. **How Environment Variables Are Loaded from .env Files**

**Loading Process** (`/testbed/src/load_dotenv.rs` lines 3-59):

```rust
pub(crate) fn load_dotenv(
  config: &Config,
  settings: &Settings,
  working_directory: &Path,
) -> RunResult<'static, BTreeMap<String, String>>
```

**Loading Strategy** (priority order):

1. **Skip if not needed** (lines 18-24): If `dotenv_load=false`, `dotenv_filename=None`, `dotenv_path=None`, and `dotenv_required=false`, return empty map.

2. **Check explicit path first** (lines 26-31): If `dotenv_path` is set, load from that specific file path (relative to working directory).

3. **Search for filename** (lines 35-40): Walk up the directory tree from `working_directory` searching for `dotenv_filename` (defaults to `.env`).

4. **Error handling** (lines 42-46): If `dotenv_required=true` and no file found, throw `Error::DotenvRequired`.

**File Loading** (lines 49-59):
```rust
fn load_from_file(path: &Path) -> RunResult<'static, BTreeMap<String, String>> {
  let iter = dotenvy::from_path_iter(path)?;  // Uses dotenvy crate
  let mut dotenv = BTreeMap::new();
  for result in iter {
    let (key, value) = result?;
    if env::var_os(&key).is_none() {  // Only set if not already in env
      dotenv.insert(key, value);
    }
  }
  Ok(dotenv)
}
```

**Key behavior**: Existing environment variables take precedence over .env values (line 54).

**Integration Point** (`/testbed/src/justfile.rs` lines 90-94):
```rust
let dotenv = if config.load_dotenv {
  load_dotenv(config, &self.settings, &search.working_directory)?
} else {
  BTreeMap::new()
};
```

**Export to Commands** (`/testbed/src/command_ext.rs` lines 22-38):
```rust
fn export(
  &mut self,
  settings: &Settings,
  dotenv: &BTreeMap<String, String>,
  scope: &Scope,
  unexports: &HashSet<String>,
) -> &mut Command {
  for (name, value) in dotenv {
    self.env(name, value);  // Sets environment variable
  }
  // ... exports scope variables
}
```

### 4. **Existing Tests for Dotenv Functionality**

**Test File**: `/testbed/tests/dotenv.rs` (410 lines)

**Key Test Categories**:

1. **Basic loading**:
   - `dotenv()` - Load root .env when subdir doesn't have one
   - `set_false()` - Disabling with `set dotenv-load := false`
   - `set_implicit()` - Implicit true with `set dotenv-load`
   - `set_true()` - Explicit true with `set dotenv-load := true`

2. **Settings tests**:
   - `dotenv_required()` - Error when file missing with `set dotenv-required`
   - `path_resolves()` - `--dotenv-path` CLI flag
   - `filename_resolves()` - `--dotenv-filename` CLI flag
   - `can_set_dotenv_filename_from_justfile()` - Setting in recipe file
   - `can_set_dotenv_path_from_justfile()` - Setting in recipe file

3. **Priority/Override tests**:
   - `program_argument_has_priority_for_dotenv_filename()` - CLI overrides justfile
   - `program_argument_has_priority_for_dotenv_path()` - CLI overrides justfile
   - `filename_flag_overwrites_no_load()` - CLI flag overrides disabled loading
   - `path_flag_overwrites_no_load()` - CLI flag overrides disabled loading

4. **Variable usage tests**:
   - `dotenv_variable_in_recipe()` - Using in recipe commands
   - `dotenv_variable_in_backtick()` - Using in backtick expressions
   - `dotenv_variable_in_function_in_recipe()` - Using with `env_var()` function
   - `dotenv_variable_in_function_in_backtick()` - Using with `env_var()` in backticks

5. **Environment override**:
   - `dotenv_env_var_override()` - OS environment variables override .env values
   - `no_dotenv()` - `--no-dotenv` CLI flag disables loading

6. **Path resolution**:
   - `dotenv_path_is_relative_to_working_directory()` - Relative to working dir
   - `dotenv_path_usable_from_subdir()` - Accessible from subdirectories

---

### **CLI Arguments** (`/testbed/src/config.rs`)

- `--dotenv-path` / `-E`: Specify custom .env file path
- `--dotenv-filename`: Specify custom .env filename
- `--no-dotenv`: Disable .env loading completely
- Environment variable: `JUST_NO_DOTENV` (affects `--no-dotenv`)

---

### **Priority Order (Lowest to Highest)**

1. Default values in `Settings::default()`
2. Values from `set` directives in justfile
3. Command-line arguments (`--dotenv-path`, `--dotenv-filename`)

This is reflected in `/testbed/src/load_dotenv.rs` lines 8-16:
```rust
let dotenv_filename = config.dotenv_filename.as_ref()
  .or(settings.dotenv_filename.as_ref());

let dotenv_path = config.dotenv_path.as_ref()
  .or(settings.dotenv_path.as_ref());
```

---

### **Key Behaviors**

1. **Environment variables take precedence**: OS env vars override .env file values
2. **Implicit true for booleans**: `set dotenv-load` equals `set dotenv-load := true`
3. **Walking directory tree**: When using filename search, searches from working directory up to root
4. **Command-line overrides**: CLI flags override justfile settings
5. **Uses `dotenvy` crate**: For actual .env file parsing

use super::*;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub(crate) enum Setting<'src> {
  AllowDuplicateRecipes(bool),
  AllowDuplicateVariables(bool),
  DotenvFilename(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvLoad(bool),
  DotenvPath(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvRequired(bool),
  Export(bool),
  Fallback(bool),
  IgnoreComments(bool),
  NoExitMessage(bool),
  PositionalArguments(bool),
  Quiet(bool),
  ScriptInterpreter(Interpreter<'src>),
  Shell(Interpreter<'src>),
  Tempdir(StringLiteral<'src>),
  Unstable(bool),
  WindowsPowerShell(bool),
  WindowsShell(Interpreter<'src>),
  WorkingDirectory(StringLiteral<'src>),
}

impl Display for Setting<'_> {
  fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter) -> fmt::Result {
    match self {
      Self::AllowDuplicateRecipes(value)
      | Self::AllowDuplicateVariables(value)
      | Self::DotenvLoad(value)
      | Self::DotenvRequired(value)
      | Self::Export(value)
      | Self::Fallback(value)
      | Self::IgnoreComments(value)
      | Self::NoExitMessage(value)
      | Self::PositionalArguments(value)
      | Self::Quiet(value)
      | Self::Unstable(value)
      | Self::WindowsPowerShell(value) => write!(f, "{value}"),
      Self::ScriptInterpreter(shell) | Self::Shell(shell) | Self::WindowsShell(shell) => {
        write!(f, "[{shell}]")
      }
      Self::DotenvFilename(value)
      | Self::DotenvPath(value)
      | Self::Tempdir(value)
      | Self::WorkingDirectory(value) => {
        write!(f, "{value}")
      }
    }
  }
}

use super::*;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub(crate) enum Setting<'src> {
  AllowDuplicateRecipes(bool),
  AllowDuplicateVariables(bool),
  DotenvFilename(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvLoad(bool),
  DotenvPath(StringLiteral<'src>),
  DotenvRequired(bool),
  Export(bool),
  Fallback(bool),
  IgnoreComments(bool),
  NoExitMessage(bool),
  PositionalArguments(bool),
  Quiet(bool),
  ScriptInterpreter(Interpreter<'src>),
  Shell(Interpreter<'src>),
  Tempdir(StringLiteral<'src>),
  Unstable(bool),
  WindowsPowerShell(bool),
  WindowsShell(Interpreter<'src>),
  WorkingDirectory(StringLiteral<'src>),
}

impl Display for Setting<'_> {
  fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter) -> fmt::Result {
    match self {
      Self::AllowDuplicateRecipes(value)
      | Self::AllowDuplicateVariables(value)
      | Self::DotenvLoad(value)
      | Self::DotenvRequired(value)
      | Self::Export(value)
      | Self::Fallback(value)
      | Self::IgnoreComments(value)

      Self::ScriptInterpreter(shell) | Self::Shell(shell) | Self::WindowsShell(shell) => {
        write!(f, "[{shell}]")
      }
      Self::DotenvFilename(value)
      | Self::DotenvPath(value)
      | Self::Tempdir(value)
      | Self::WorkingDirectory(value) => {
        write!(f, "{value}")
      }
    }
  }
}
